title: 'Pragmata' director Yonghee Cho on creating Capcom’s resonant sci-fi lunar adventure (interview)
summary: The director of 'Pragmata', Yonghee Cho, discusses the creation of Capcom's upcoming sci-fi lunar adventure game. The project is described as a challenging yet exciting creative endeavor.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 209 — Astronauts for America
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' features a discussion with Garrett Reisman and Steve Lindsey, leaders of the new organization 'Astronauts for America'. The podcast episode focuses on their initiatives and perspectives.

title: NASA's twin Voyager spacecraft are very low on power after nearly 50 years. How long can they keep going?
summary: NASA's Voyager spacecraft, launched in 1977, are running low on power after nearly 50 years in interstellar space. It remains uncertain how much longer they can continue operating.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 commander and astrophotographer team up to capture breathtaking, never-before-seen shots of the moon's far side
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman and astrophotographer Andrew McCarthy will collaborate to capture unprecedented images of the moon's far side. This partnership aims to produce breathtaking visuals of a rarely seen lunar region.

title: Pentagon unveils trove of declassfied 'UFO' videos. How to see them all, from 'a football-shaped body' to 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light'
summary: The Pentagon released nearly 30 declassified UFO videos, including footage of objects described as a 'football-shaped body' and 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light.' These files were unveiled on May 8 as part of a new tranche of UFO-related documents.

title: Black holes slamming into scorching stars may be causing mysterious blue flashes in the cosmos
summary: Scientists propose that Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients (LFBOTs), bright blue cosmic explosions, may result from black holes or neutron stars colliding with the universe's hottest stars. This theory could explain the origin of these mysterious and powerful phenomena.

title: Monopoly sucks, but this new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff might add some galactic sizzle
summary: A new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff is generating excitement in the gaming community, drawing parallels to the strategic elements of the board game Monopoly. The game is expected to bring fresh galactic-themed gameplay to fans.

title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch for the 34th Commercial Resupply Mission to the International Space Station, carrying about 6,500 pounds of supplies aboard a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft on a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a colorized image of Mars during a gravity assist approach on May 3, 2026. The spacecraft will use the gravity assist on May 15 to adjust its trajectory toward asteroid Psyche.

title: I Am Artemis: Anton Kiriwas
summary: Anton Kiriwas, a senior technical integration manager for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, shares his journey from being inspired by images of the Moon and Mars to working on Artemis missions. His story highlights NASA's efforts to engage and inspire the next generation of space professionals.

title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
summary: NASA has been advancing high-performance spaceflight computing for decades, starting with the Apollo Guidance Computers in the 1960s. These radiation-hardened processors remain essential for spacecraft operations.

title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured the Milky Way rising above Earth's atmospheric glow, known as airglow, from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft docked to the International Space Station on April 13, 2026. The phenomenon occurs when excited atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere emit light to release excess energy.

title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
summary: NASA appointed Brian Hughes as senior director of launch operations at Kennedy Space Center. He will oversee launch infrastructure and report to NASA Headquarters.

title: NASA Fuel Cell Tests Pave Way for Energy Storage on Moon
summary: NASA conducted fuel cell tests to advance energy storage solutions for lunar missions. The cylindrical fuel cell, equipped with numerous sensors and tubes, was carefully installed for evaluation.

title: Tracy Arm’s Post-Tsunami Landscape
summary: A landslide-triggered tsunami in summer 2025 stripped vegetation from the shore of Tracy Arm, a glacial fjord. This event highlights the impact of natural disasters on remote glacial landscapes.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 supersonic jets and a Pilatus PC-12 utility plane, continuing its legacy in flight research. The center supports science and aeronautics test missions, building on the work of pioneers like Neil Armstrong.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords, joining the international coalition for civil space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Paraguay's commitment during a ceremony in Asunción.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 awaits new date for WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 is awaiting a new date for its Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 completed a successful static fire test on May 7, 2026. The next steps depend on the results and scheduling of the WDR.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion to support Flight 12, which is nearing readiness six months after Flight 11 concluded. The upgrade work aims to prepare the launch site for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before increased activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its twelfth Starship flight test, with an updated flight trajectory. The return of Starship launches is anticipated within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A doctor with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service has been selected as the next NASA astronaut for an upcoming ISS mission. The astronaut discussed their preparation and the mission's objectives.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute system underwent a critical 'bake-out' test to ensure its reliability. The process involves heating the parachute to remove moisture and contaminants before deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 descent to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed for space applications, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and debris removal. This innovation could enhance the safety and efficiency of in-space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, including an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully transmitted from an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the navigation technology being developed.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round and unveiled a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in supporting NASA's lunar exploration initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, rebranding it as Rocket Lab Robotics upon completion in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. This follows the company's S-1 filing on May 1, which set the price range at $24-$26.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging technology as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to enhance EO capabilities with its advanced thermal imaging solutions.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is aiming to commercialize quantum research, positioning itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is focusing on advancing quantum technologies to drive economic and scientific growth.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy, addressing potential fuel line vulnerabilities. The company aims to mitigate threats to fuel deliveries by leveraging solar power harnessed in space.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a new funding round, including a $300 million Series E led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton. The company's valuation reached $2.8 billion according to a source close to the deal.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding to expand operations, including building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington D.C. and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation, aiming to scale its business and meet rising demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer, aiming to act as a centralized hub. This initiative seeks to simplify regulatory processes for emerging space applications.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. Planet Labs Germany will provide numerous satellite data solutions under this agreement.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, where models must maintain performance across diverse and real-world conditions. This shift highlights the growing challenges and limitations of current Earth observation data.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 amid its ongoing acquisition process by Amazon. The satellite operator's financial performance reflects its strategic position during the acquisition talks.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026 and highlighted progress on its Neutron launch vehicle, along with a robotics acquisition and an SBI deal.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The Indian launch startup is a rising star in the country's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after securing significant funding.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites per year. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported a 32% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, with its satellite manufacturing business seeing over 40% growth. CEO Mike Greenley noted that the Globalstar deal did not impact these results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to address evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is focused on resilience and adopting emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.
